title: schedonorus arundinaceus subsp. corsicus
intro: Schedonorus arundinaceus subsp. corsicus, also known as Corsican bent or Schedonorus corsicus, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the Mediterranean region. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, valued for its diuretic and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, offering health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and enhancing immune function. In herbal preparations, Schedonorus arundinaceus subsp. corsicus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
